headline: OpenAI Completes GPT-4o Retirement, Ending Phased Sunset of Legacy Frontier Model
raw_snippet: OpenAI completed the full retirement of GPT-4o across all ChatGPT plans on April 3, closing out a phased sunset that began February 13, 2026. The move consolidates consumer tiers on GPT-5.4 as OpenAI prepares for the forthcoming GPT-5.5 release.

headline: Federal Judge Affirms Order Requiring OpenAI to Release 20 Million Anonymized ChatGPT Logs in Copyright Case
raw_snippet: US District Judge Sidney Stein affirmed a magistrate judge's order requiring OpenAI to produce 20 million anonymized ChatGPT conversation logs in the consolidated AI copyright litigation brought by The New York Times, Chicago Tribune, and other publishers. The court rejected OpenAI's privacy objections, finding the logs relevant to plaintiffs' fair-use analysis even where they do not directly reproduce copyrighted works.

headline: OpenAI Publishes 'Inside Our Approach to the Model Spec' Detailing AI Behavior Framework
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a detailed explanation of its Model Spec — the public framework that defines how OpenAI's models adjudicate between obligations to users, developers, and OpenAI itself. The post details the new authority hierarchy (Root > System > Developer > User > Guideline) and updated principles for agentic actions and teen-user safety.
curator_notes: Important framework document for AI policy/compliance discussion.
